blockers.description: Scanner still shows 'getUserMedia is not defined' error after trying multiple fix approaches

human_actions_pending.action: Test scanner in browser after latest fix (CDN script tag approach)
human_actions_pending.context: Need to verify if loading Quagga via CDN script tag in app.html resolves the SSR issue

decisions.decision: Load Quagga via CDN script tag instead of npm import
decisions.rationale: To avoid SSR issues with Quagga's browser-only dependencies

decisions.decision: Use Open Library API instead of Google Books
decisions.rationale: Google Books has rate limits, Open Library is free

next_action: Test scanner in browser - the latest fix loads Quagga via CDN script tag in app.html
context_notes: Been debugging scanner for several iterations. Tried: (1) dynamic import in onMount, (2) ssr.external in vite config, (3) CDN script tag approach. All resulted in same getUserMedia error. The error occurs when Quagga.init() is called - it seems Quagga is still being loaded/processed during SSR despite our guards.
